result_summary: The shell/CLI extension direction is coherent, grounded in the existing metadata model, demonstrated with validated examples, compatibility-classified as non-breaking at the base-schema level, and now backed by an explicit schema-backed extension-constraint layer. The proposal is ready to move beyond the proposal register toward amendment review.
passes: existing metadata model is kept as the authority baseline
shell assets are classified as projection assets
implementation backends are classified as projection targets
verification backends are classified as projection consumers
shell policy and inventory are aligned with the projection-only posture
a shell_cli extension profile has been defined against the existing canonical semantic model
an example canonical semantic model for shell_cli validates against the existing metadata schema
an example projection artifact manifest for shell_cli validates against the existing metadata schema
compatibility assessment classifies the current profile as non-breaking to the base metadata schemas
schema-backed extension constraints exist and validate
a first amendment-facing slice is defined for Bashly implementation projection and Bats verification projection
Bashly is reduced to adapter primitives, required config surfaces, and matrixed mappings against the metadata profile
underspecified_items: follow-on backend slices remain for argc, Argbash, and ShellSpec
next_actions: carry the Bashly and Bats slice into amendment review
define additional backend projection examples as separate follow-on slices
